title: Legal Aid Bureau
text:
Legal Aid Bureau - SupportGoWhere
Legal Aid Bureau
Share link
Provides legal help for eligible individuals in various civil proceedings.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Legal representation for qualified applicants in various civil proceedings
Assistance with drafting Wills and Deeds of Separation
Free Legal advice for qualified applicants in various civil proceedings
Apply now
Collapse all sections
About this support

Legal advice on Singapore law and practical steps for your case.

Assistance in drafting simple legal documents like Wills and Deeds of Separation.

Legal representation in various civil proceedings, subject to eligibility.

Who is this for?

Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents residing in Singapore




Eligibility Tests:

Means Test for Legal Advice and Assistance

Means and Merits Test for Legal Aid

Duty Lawyer available for consultation and assessment of eligibility for legal aid.

How to apply?

Register case online with the step by step guide on the Applicant Portal

Attend the Legal Aid Bureau (LAB) within three working days after online registration

Schedule an appointment via the OneMinLaw Portal before visiting the Minlaw Services Centre at 45 Maxwell Road #07-11, the URA Centre (East Wing)

If you're a minor or representing someone without mental incapacity, a parent/guardian, or Court-appointed Deputy must apply on your behalf respectively.

Where can I find help?

View more details

Ministry of Law Hotline: 1800 2255 529

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

45 Maxwell Road #07-11, The URA Centre (East Wing), Singapore 069118

Scheme last updated 30 Apr 2026
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
Answer a few questions to find schemes and services you may be eligible for.
